content:
# Tiered Knowledge Schema Foundation — Implementation Record (2026-03-27)

## What Was Implemented (Issue #14, PR #29)

Schema foundation for tiered knowledge lifecycle management, merged to master.

### Types Added (corvia-common/src/types.rs)
- `MemoryType` enum: Structural, Decisional, Episodic (default), Analytical, Procedural
  - Derives: Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize
  - Has Display, FromStr, serde rename_all=snake_case
- `Tier` enum: Hot (default), Warm, Cold, Forgotten
  - Same derives plus PartialOrd, Ord (natural ordering: Hot > Warm > Cold > Forgotten)
  - Variant order in enum is reversed (Forgotten first) to get correct Ord derivation
- `PinInfo` struct: { by: String, at: DateTime<Utc> }
  - Replaces 3 separate pin fields — makes invalid states unrepresentable

### KnowledgeEntry Fields Added
memory_type, confidence (Option<f32>, clamped 0-1), last_accessed, access_count (u32, saturating), tier, tier_changed_at, retention_score (Option<f32>), pin (Option<PinInfo>). All #[serde(default)].

### Key API Methods
- `MemoryType::from_content_role(Option<&str>)` — auto-classification via match
- `MemoryType::is_known_content_role(&str)` — delegates to try_from_role
- `with_confidence(f32)` — clamps to [0,1], rejects NaN
- `record_access()` — saturating_add + timestamp
- `pin(by)` / `unpin()` / `is_pinned()` — safe pinning API

### Design Decisions Made During Review
1. PinInfo struct over 3 separate fields (SWE + API + Rust reviewers agreed)
2. Tier has PartialOrd/Ord with reversed variant order for natural ordering
3. classify_memory_type uses match (not linear scan array) with shared try_from_role
4. tracing dependency added to corvia-common for unmapped content_role warnings
5. Free functions kept as thin wrappers over associated methods for backward compat
